blocks.content: Master of Consciousness (3)

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Do not threaten our peace!
The speed of the massive axe descending towards her head was almost impossible to follow with the eyes.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: In that fleeting moment, Amy realized this was her end.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Goodbye, Shirone...
It was a bad ending.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: But in a way, she thought it might not be a bad conclusion. This was Shirone's mental world. She would sleep forever in his embrace.
Raaaargh!
A ferocious roar snapped Amy back to reality. At the same time, something dark and fast moved past her eyes.
When she came to her senses, Ignite had been thrown 10 meters away.
Amy felt a presence behind her and turned around.
But to see it properly, she had to tilt her head all the way back.
It was an Egoist, covered in spiky protrusions all over its body. It stood over 6 meters tall, in a hunched posture that was somewhere between quadrupedal and bipedal, glaring forward.
The Egoist manifested in a specific form, borrowing the avatar's desire.
The process wasn't finished yet, and all the Egoists in the plaza turned into black smoke and were absorbed into it.
Although it didn't grow taller, the spikes on its body became abnormally long.
Raaaargh!
Egoist - Rage Form.
The giant shadowy monster roared and leaped over Amy.
With the obstruction gone, she saw Shirone with a fierce expression.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Shirone...?
Shirone had a stronger inclination towards reason than desire. But the current Egoist was exuding a strength on a different level than anything she had experienced before.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Could it be that he's trying to save me?
Knowing the hostility Shirone's avatar held towards her, Amy avoided jumping to conclusions.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: But she couldn't help but feel a swelling hope. It had always been this way. Whenever danger struck, Shirone always protected her.
The Egoist (Rage Form) tore through hundreds of antitheses and still had energy to spare.
Even Ignite's heavily armed subordinates were sent flying with a single kick.

blocks.speaker: Ignite
blocks.content: Shirone! Do you still dream of a petty life to the end?
Ignite, coughing up blood, grabbed his massive axe and charged at the monster.
He leaped high and swung the axe behind him.
Bending his back like a bow, he sprang forward and brought the axe down.
The Egoist's head was split in half.
A smile formed on Ignite's lips, but the expected scream didn't come.
The state of the Egoist transmitted through the handle was still angry, bubbling, and about to explode.
As Ignite's expression hardened, the Rage Form's claw swiped across his face.
With half his face gone, he fell to the ground, and a leg thicker than a pillar stomped on him.
Boom!
The ground shook, signaling the end of the battle.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Ahhh! R-run away!
The antitheses scattered in all directions in panic. The Egoist roared and chased after them, trying to eliminate even one more.
The plaza, now empty except for Shirone's party, was clean without a single corpse.
Amy, who had been on the brink of death, collapsed to the ground.
Shirone approached her.
Turning her head, Amy's heart raced as she realized Shirone was there.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Was his anger gone? Had he returned to the kind Shirone of before?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone, thank you. If it weren't for you...

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You make me angry.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You really make me angry.
Then he turned coldly and walked away.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Even now, he couldn't understand why he had saved Amy.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Just before the massive axe split her head, he was seized by an intense impulse. From that moment, his rage exploded, and he couldn't remember anything else.
Amy hung her head in dejection.
Reina, who had hoped for their reconciliation, patted her shoulder.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Don't be too disappointed. Once we save Shirone, all misunderstandings will...
Before Reina could finish, Amy lifted her head.
Contrary to expectations, her face was bright. She even looked somewhat relieved.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: It's okay. Shirone saved me, after all. Maybe he doesn't hate me to the core. That's enough for me. Haha!

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Even though she said that, Reina could guess how great Amy's sense of loss was.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: She was holding on desperately, solely to save Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Yeah, let's keep going a little longer.
Reina looked at Armin.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Are the antitheses gone now?

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: They're not gone. But the manipulated antitheses are no more. Still, we can't let our guard down. They are fundamentally mental entities that criticize Shirone.
After a short rest in the plaza, they followed Shirone's lead into the castle.
Although Ignite had been there just a while ago, no projections were visible anymore.
Shirone descended into the basement.
He walked through the maze-like paths without a single hesitation.
When he opened the door to the storeroom, another staircase leading down appeared.
On the first basement floor, Armin grabbed a torch and walked alongside Shirone.
At an old wooden door, he stopped the group and turned around.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Beyond this door is the world of dreams, the REM realm. Before we enter, there are a few things to note.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: You've all dreamed before, but different physical laws apply here than in reality. Simply put, it's called focus.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Is it like concentration?

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Similar. Reality is a series of concentration and relaxation. In an ordinary life, the relaxation phase is overwhelmingly long. But in dreams, there's only concentration. It's like following the dramatic structure of a play. You only see what you want to see, and other situations don't exist. So if you lose focus, you lose yourself. This is called 'rilpin'.
Armin held up five fingers.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: There are five main types of rilpin. First, a sense of floating. Second, meaningless mumbling. Third, repetition of the same segment. Fourth, perspective shift. Fifth, the realization of anxiety.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Amy thought she understood. She had often experienced these things while dreaming.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Dreams only mimic gravity; they don't actually have it. Keep confirming the feeling of your feet being on the ground. The moment you feel a sense of floating, you'll keep rising endlessly. And don't speak without certainty.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Conversations need focus too, like a script in a play.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Exactly. Habitual slang is also not allowed. Losing focus in language will make you repeat meaningless words.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: So the repetition of the same segment is spatial focus?

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Yes. Don't turn around or run away without thinking. You'll lose focus and repeat the same segment. Fourth, if you focus on the surrounding situation, you'll lose yourself and become an observer. Lastly, don't have anxious thoughts.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Because they'll become reality.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: As long as we don't all get caught at the same time, we can pull each other out. If you start floating, we'll pull you down. If you start mumbling, we might slap you.
Amy puffed out her cheeks as if she had already been slapped.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: What if we can't get out?

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Eventually, you'll catch a new focus. But it won't be Shirone's focus; it will be ours. In the end, you'll be dreaming a new dream.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: A dream within a dream.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Exactly. This is why Drimo is called a multidimensional world. It's fine in your own dream, but this is someone else's world. If you get deeply buried, you might never wake up from the dream.
Everyone tensed up after hearing Armin's explanation.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Don't worry. As long as we focus on following Shirone to the next exit, there shouldn't be any problems.
Shirone took a deep breath and grabbed the doorknob.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then... I'll open the door.
With Armin's approval, the door slowly opened.
Music softly played, and soon the performance of court musicians filled the air.
Amy squinted at the familiar scene.
This place...?

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Many couples were dancing in the grand hall. It was the ballroom of Kazra Castle, where they had been just a few hours ago.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: The REM realm is a fluid space. It's like multiple plays being performed on the same stage. If the background is the grand hall, the exit should be close.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's the exit.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Amy thought she understood why. It was the door she had exited with Zion. Shirone must have gone through that door to find her. And then all this happened.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: It was because of me.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Haha, no wonder he hates me.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Amy, don't lose focus.
Amy quickly regained her composure.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Yes, I'm ready.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then let's go. Shirone, walk straight ahead without bumping into anyone.
Shirone walked carefully.
He stopped and waited when couples passed by.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Taking a detour could lead to rilpin.
Amy and Reina only looked at the back of Shirone's head.
Meanwhile, Armin walked leisurely, observing the surroundings.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: He had obtained a lucid dream certification for a certain mission, making this possible.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: What exactly happened?

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Given that Shirone in reality was facing a horrific death, the REM realm's situation was likely a nightmare.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: This made him tense.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: At any moment, everyone in the ballroom could turn into monsters, or people might suddenly go mad and start killing each other.
However, nothing happened as they crossed half of the ballroom.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Since the focus had already shifted from the path they had taken, it was unlikely that anything would happen there.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Strange. What kind of nightmare is this?
Armin noticed something and his eyes lit up.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: So, that's what it is.
In the center of the ballroom, a couple was dancing.
The woman wore a beautiful red dress like a rose, and the man had a strange appearance.
The man's eyes were completely slanted upwards, his nose was upturned, and his lips were smaller than a chicken's anus.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: It was a human face, but one that couldn't exist in reality.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: He must have really hated him.
If it were just this, it wouldn't be a big deal for a third party.
But for Shirone, it was a horrific nightmare.
Armin turned his gaze and saw someone serving near a long table.
He was dressed in the attire of a steppe tribe, which didn't match the castle's scenery at all.
He wore a red hat, had a stylishly long beard, and was confidently smoking a pipe even in a non-smoking area.
The only entity not a projection of Shirone, he was Luber, a dreamer dispatched from Drimo.
Luber looked at Armin and winked.
But Armin just glanced at him indifferently and turned away.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: He wasn't someone to greet out of mere curiosity.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: Currently, dreamers couldn't be analyzed by human intelligence.

blocks.speaker: Armin
blocks.content: They were acknowledged simply because they existed.
When Shirone reached the exit, the focus on the path they had taken ended, and the music stopped.
Was the ball still going on in the grand hall? They were curious, but no one looked back.
